entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop [Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp.] traditionally photoperiod-sensitive.

entities.name: PURE LINE BREEDING
entities.description: A resource-intensive breeding activity used to develop new cultivars.

entities.name: OFF-SEASON NURSERIES
entities.description: A technique used to reduce the length of the breeding cycle in some crops.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD-SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The requirement for long periods of darkness to induce flowering.

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The process of producing flowers, induced by darkness in photoperiod-sensitive pigeonpea.

entities.name: EARLY MATURING
entities.description: A characteristic of genotypes that reach maturity faster.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD-INSENSITIVITY
entities.description: The ability of genotypes to flower regardless of day length.

entities.name: SPEED BREEDING
entities.description: An approach to enable rapid generation turnover and accelerate genetic gain.

entities.name: RAPID GENERATION TURNOVER
entities.description: The process of producing multiple generations of a crop per year.

entities.name: IMMATURE SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: A technique used for rapid generation advancement.

entities.name: SINGLE POD DESCENT
entities.description: A breeding method used to accelerate line development while conserving genetic variability.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The diversity of genetic characteristics within a population.

entities.name: HOMOZYGOUS LINES
entities.description: Lines that are genetically uniform, resulting from the breeding process.

entities.name: GENETIC GAIN
entities.description: The increase in the average value of a trait in a population over time.

Requires long periods of darkness to induce
Used to develop new cultivars of
Reduces the length of the breeding cycle
